verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the F1β subunit of ATP synthase, resident in the inner mitochondrial membrane with its catalytic domain facing the mitochondrial matrix. It has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, signal peptide, or secretory pathway access. No documented stress-induced, cancer-associated, cell-death-related, activation-induced, or tissue-restricted outer-leaflet pool. Not a target of surface-directed therapeutics. No lysosomal-exocytosis link, no PM-cycling dual localization, and no covalent TM-partner tethering. Strictly mitochondrial-compartment resident.
